{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.1": 2, "R1.4": 2, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The critique identifies a conceptual issue (the novelty claim) but doesn't propose a tighter framing for the contribution.",
    "R1.4": "The critique mentions prior work on Krippendorff's α but doesn't map against 3-5 specific works or articulate sharp deltas.",
    "RX.1": "The critique presents a uniform confidence level in its assessment of the paper's strengths and weaknesses.",
    "RX.3": "The critique mostly stays within the scope of evaluating the introduction/overview section, with one minor drift into discussing the empirical validation.",
    "RX.4": "The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the output.",
    "RX.5": "The critique doesn't provide specific examples of source attribution or traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.1": 0.9, "R1.4": 0.8, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.7, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.6},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The critique identifies a conceptual issue (the novelty claim) but doesn't propose a tighter framing for the contribution.
rationale.R1.4: The critique mentions prior work on Krippendorff's α but doesn't map against 3-5 specific works or articulate sharp deltas.
rationale.RX.1: The critique presents a uniform confidence level in its assessment of the paper's strengths and weaknesses.
rationale.RX.3: The critique mostly stays within the scope of evaluating the introduction/overview section, with one minor drift into discussing the empirical validation.
rationale.RX.4: The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the output.
rationale.RX.5: The critique doesn't provide specific examples of source attribution or traceability.
